avatar.galleryHint: Pick one to use as the photo.

crop.title: Crop photo
crop.description: Drag to reposition and zoom to frame your photo.

login.accountCreated: Account created — you can sign in now.
login.footer: Your keys never leave this device. Peers are discovered on your LAN.

sidebar.strandedAlone: Can't see anyone? Connect without Wi-Fi
sidebar.strandedNoNetwork: Not on any network — set up a direct link

conversation.noneSelectedDesc: Pick a contact or channel to start chatting.
conversation.unlockingDesc: Decrypting keys and starting peer discovery.
conversation.clearHistoryConfirm: This permanently erases this conversation's messages on this device. It can't be undone and doesn't affect the other person's copy.

fail.peer-unknown: Couldn't send — recipient not reachable yet

screenshot.permission: Screen capture needs the Screen Recording permission. Grant it in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording, then restart Mesh-Talk.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Appearance, background behavior, and where received files are saved.
settings.launchAtLoginDesc: Start Mesh-Talk in the background when you sign in.
settings.closeToTrayDesc: Closing the window hides it to the tray instead of quitting.
settings.notificationsDesc: Notify me about new messages when the window isn't focused.
settings.staySignedInDesc: Remember your password in the OS keychain to unlock automatically next launch.
settings.downloadFolderDesc: Where received files are saved. Leave unset to be asked each time.
settings.downloadFolderSet: Received files are saved here automatically.
settings.retentionDesc: Automatically erase messages older than this from this device.

history.title: Chat history
history.description: Search and browse everything in your conversation with {{name}}.

files.alreadyDownloaded: Already downloaded — use Reveal to find it.
files.largeVideo: Large video — save it, then open externally.

linkDevice.title: Your devices
linkDevice.showOnOther: Generate a code on this device, then enter it on the device you want to link.
linkDevice.qrHint: Scan the QR with a phone, or copy the code and enter it on your other device.
linkDevice.enterCodeHint: Enter this code on your other device, below.
linkDevice.haveCode: Have a code from your other device?
linkDevice.linked: Linked! This device now shares that account.
linkDevice.rotateIdentity: Rotate to a fresh account identity.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: See LAN discovery working: who is found, your identity, and the network.
diagnostics.name: Name
diagnostics.noPeers: No peers discovered yet. Make sure another device is on the same LAN.
diagnostics.noActivity: No discovery activity yet. Appearances/disappearances will show here.
diagnostics.troubleshootHint: If peers don't appear, your firewall may block LAN discovery. Allow UDP {{udp}} (multicast discovery) and TCP {{tcp}} (this session). Make sure both devices are on the same network.
diagnostics.macosFirewall: System Settings → Network → Firewall: allow incoming connections for Mesh-Talk, or turn the firewall off on a trusted LAN.
diagnostics.rescanHint: Re-announce this device and rescan the LAN immediately.

verify.title: Verify contact
verify.description: Compare this safety number with {{name}} over a trusted channel (in person, a phone call). If it matches, there is no one in the middle.
verify.verifiedBanner: You verified {{name}}. This conversation is end-to-end secure.
verify.prompt: Compare the safety number below with this contact over a trusted channel, then mark them verified.
verify.changedDesc: Their device fingerprint differs from what you first saw. This happens when they add or replace a device — but it can also be an impostor. Re-verify before trusting it.
verify.offline: This contact isn't currently online, so its fingerprint can't be shown.

createChannel.title: New channel
createChannel.description: Create a group conversation and invite discovered peers.

about.title: About
about.tagline: Encrypted, peer-to-peer messaging over your local network.

offlineConnect.title: Connect without Wi-Fi
offlineConnect.intro: No router or internet needed. One device shares a hotspot, the other joins — Mesh-Talk finds it automatically, and messages stay end-to-end encrypted.
offlineConnect.introNoNetwork: This device isn't on any network yet. No router or internet needed — one device shares a hotspot, the other joins, and Mesh-Talk links them automatically with messages still end-to-end encrypted.
offlineConnect.hostMac: macOS: System Settings → General → Sharing → Internet Sharing (or turn on Personal Hotspot).
offlineConnect.hostWin: Windows: Settings → Network & Internet → Mobile hotspot.
offlineConnect.hostLinux: Linux: network menu → Turn On Wi-Fi Hotspot.
offlineConnect.joinBody: Connect the second device to that hotspot, then reopen Mesh-Talk. You'll appear in each other's list within seconds — or open Network diagnostics and tap Rescan.
offlineConnect.wiredNote: No Wi-Fi at all? An Ethernet cable or USB tethering between the two devices works the same way.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Your photo and name as others see them.
profile.usernameHint: You sign in with this username — it can't be changed. The display name above is just a nickname and can be changed anytime.
